title: Fort William
tagline: Fort William — heritage city of West Bengal

overview.short: Fort William, officially Vijay Durg, is a fort in Hastings, Calcutta (Kolkata).
overview.description: Fort William, officially Vijay Durg, is a fort in Hastings, Calcutta (Kolkata). It was built during the early years of Britain's administration of Bengal. It sits on the eastern banks of the River Hooghly, the major distributary of the River Ganga. One of Kolkata's most enduring British-era military fortifications, other than those in Bombay (Mumbai) and Madras (Chennai), it extends over an area of seventy hectares.

nearestAirport.name: Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Gwalior Monument
topPlaces.description: The Gwalior Monument, also known as Ellenborough's Folly, or The Pepperpot, is an octagonal cenotaph about 18 metres (60 ft) high, crowned with a bronze dome cast from guns captured from the Scindia forces. It was erected in 1847 by Lord Ellenborough, the Governor-General of India, as a memorial to the officers and men who fell during the Gwalior War in 1843.

topPlaces.name: East Bengal FC
topPlaces.description: East Bengal Football Club, commonly referred to as East Bengal, is an Indian professional football club based in Kolkata. The club competes in the Indian Super League, the top flight of the Indian football league system.

topPlaces.name: East Bengal Club
topPlaces.description: East Bengal Club, commonly referred to as East Bengal (Bengali pronunciation: [ˈi:st ˌbenˈɡɔːl]), is an Indian professional multi-sports club based in Kolkata, West Bengal. It is best known for its professional men's football team that competes in the Indian Super League (ISL), the top flight of the Indian football league system.

topPlaces.name: East Bengal Ground
topPlaces.description: The East Bengal Ground, also known as East Bengal–Aryan Ground, is the home stadium of East Bengal and Aryan. The stadium has a capacity of 23,500 people.

topPlaces.name: Mohun Bagan Ground
topPlaces.description: Mohun Bagan Ground, also known as Mohun Bagan–Calcutta FC Ground, is a football stadium located in Maidan, Kolkata, just opposite Eden Gardens. Due to certain restrictions by the Indian Armed Forces, who own all the land surrounding Fort William, the ground is legally leased to Mohun Bagan AC for their operations.

topPlaces.name: Prinsep Ghat
topPlaces.description: Princep Ghat is a ghat built in 1841 during the British Raj, along the Kolkata bank of the Hooghly River in India. The Palladian porch in the memory of the eminent Anglo-Indian scholar and antiquary James Prinsep was designed by W. Fitzgerald and constructed in 1843.

topPlaces.name: Outram Ghat
topPlaces.description: Outram Ghat is a ghat built during British Raj, in Kolkata along the river bank of Hooghly, a distributary of the Ganges River. It was built in late nineteenth century by British authorities in memory of Sir James Outram.

topPlaces.name: Football Lovers' Day
topPlaces.description: Football Lovers' Day is observed in memorial to be the most tragic day in Indian football history, when 16 football fans died due to a stampede and riot inside the Eden Gardens stadium in Kolkata on 16 August 1980 on the occasion of a Kolkata Derby match in the Calcutta Football League.

items.note: The Gwalior Monument, also known as Ellenborough's Folly, or The Pepperpot, is an octagonal cenotaph about 18 metres (60 ft) high, crowned with a bronze dome cast from guns captured from the Scindia forces. It was erected in 1847 by Lord Ellenborough, the Governor-General of India, as a memorial to the officers and men who fell during the Gwalior War in 1843.

East Bengal Football Club, commonly referred to as East Bengal, is an Indian professional football club based in Kolkata. The club competes in the Indian Super League, the top flight of the Indian football league system.

East Bengal Club, commonly referred to as East Bengal (Bengali pronunciation: [ˈi:st ˌbenˈɡɔːl]), is an Indian professional multi-sports club based in Kolkata, West Bengal. It is best known for its professional men's football team that competes in the Indian Super League (ISL), the top flight of the Indian football league system.
The East Bengal Ground, also known as East Bengal–Aryan Ground, is the home stadium of East Bengal and Aryan. The stadium has a capacity of 23,500 people.

Mohun Bagan Ground, also known as Mohun Bagan–Calcutta FC Ground, is a football stadium located in Maidan, Kolkata, just opposite Eden Gardens. Due to certain restrictions by the Indian Armed Forces, who own all the land surrounding Fort William, the ground is legally leased to Mohun Bagan AC for their operations.

Princep Ghat is a ghat built in 1841 during the British Raj, along the Kolkata bank of the Hooghly River in India. The Palladian porch in the memory of the eminent Anglo-Indian scholar and antiquary James Prinsep was designed by W. Fitzgerald and constructed in 1843.

Outram Ghat is a ghat built during British Raj, in Kolkata along the river bank of Hooghly, a distributary of the Ganges River. It was built in late nineteenth century by British authorities in memory of Sir James Outram.

Football Lovers' Day is observed in memorial to be the most tragic day in Indian football history, when 16 football fans died due to a stampede and riot inside the Eden Gardens stadium in Kolkata on 16 August 1980 on the occasion of a Kolkata Derby match in the Calcutta Football League.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Fort William?
faq.a: The best time to visit Fort William is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Fort William?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (16 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Fort William?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Fort William at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Fort William?
faq.a: Stays in Fort William start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Fort William?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gwalior Monument, East Bengal FC, East Bengal Club, East Bengal Ground, Mohun Bagan Ground.
